data.name: tbPattern.Text
data.value:
Hello! This is a little tutorial on how to use all the features throughout the app. You can toggle this tutorial anytime by clicking on the green "?" button in the top right. ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
* To start, you can click the example pattern from the grid. It will display in this textbox. Because it uses the Row Count feature, the current row will display as well.
* The Row Counter is the button located in the top left. For every pattern, you can keep track of which row you're working on. This feature isn't required, but it is very useful for keeping track of your progress.
* By typing which row you want to go to in the Change Row text box, you can jump around the rows however you like.
* To add a new pattern, use the "Add" button on the bottom left. If you choose to use the Row Counter feature, make sure to follow the format for any rows you want to be included (A line starting with capital "R" and any number will be counted. Other notes aren't counted as rows.).
Format Example: R1: mr6 R2: inc 6 (this is a note :D) R3-5: sc 12 R6: tie off
Please note that this feature doesn't account for rows that repeat, such as R3-5. So, if you wanted to go to R6, you would actually need to change the row to 4.
* To edit a pattern, use the "Edit" button. Select which pattern you want to change from the grid, then click the button. Changing the pattern will update the existing file while changing the name will make a copy.
* To delete a pattern, select the pattern from the grid and then click the "Delete" button. It will double check that you want to delete.
* You can sort your patterns by using the "Sort By" dropdown. There are four options, consisting of alphabetical ascending and descending, and date added ascending and descending.
* The arrow buttons at the bottom are used to navigate the pages of patterns you may have.
* The other window that opened is the Ravelry Patterns list. Here, there are patterns that you can use if you don't want to write your own, many free! You can easily visit the websites by selecting a pattern and clicking the "Go To Pattern" button. Also, you can close this list and revisit it by clicking the "Find Patterns" button.
* To save, you can click the Save button, but know that just clicking "Exit" will save for you. Also, your patterns are saved every time you add, edit, or switch patterns.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ Thank you so much for using my crochet helper! I hope you get lots of use out of it. :D Made by Brandy Guzman
